client_updates.highlight: Zeam: proof merging taking too long on normal hardware; i7 (20-core/28-thread) meets 0.4–0.5s target; optimizations and early type-1 aggregation in previous slot being explored
Ream: excessive memory usage (up to 28 GB) on DevNet 4; new allocator, improved pruning, and block processing fixes underway before moving to DevNet 5
Lantern: snappy decoding error root cause found and fixed; block building averages 0.9s (aggregation only, excludes final block proof)
Ethlambda: unified branch sync, fork-choice/finality alignment with lean spec; Gloas prototype started for DevNet 6 proposal

testing_progress.highlight: DevNet 5: type-2 final block proof taking 2.5–2.6s on normal machines; exceeds 4s slot budget for 35% of Grandine's builds, causing late proposals and bumpy finalization
Three options discussed to address proof timing: bump machine specs to i7-class, increase slot time to 12s, or optimize type-2 proving; optimization headroom uncertain
Current DevNet 5 branch uses full-size XMSS signatures; smaller XMSS variant in progress may improve proving times

documentation.highlight: lean spec simplification nearly complete: unused functions removed, documentation condensed, more tests added; slides being prepared to share with EF this week
Plonky3: multilinear proving system skeleton merged; incremental push toward multilinear backend alongside existing univariate FRI system

eip_proposals_devnet6.highlight: Ethlambda proposing Gloas prototype for DevNet 6 consideration

action_items.action: Client teams review lean spec PR #791 (block building algorithm) for implementation guidance on attestation scoring and proof-aware block construction
Post async message to channel for Emil on type-2 proof optimization headroom and slot timing options

decisions.decision: lean spec PR #791 (optimized block building) will not be merged into spec as-is; clients encouraged to use it as implementation guidance; may be added as a separate named optional function
